message.content:
You are an OPUS BUILDER executing ONE phase of Sol's authoritative build plan for POLARIS's fabrication-safety validator.

PLAN FILE (read it first): /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md
YOUR PHASE: P1 — "Finish and harden the six foundation edits"
ALREADY-LANDED PHASES (do not redo): none yet
REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel

DO EXACTLY THIS:
1. Read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md. Find the section for phase P1, and also read "## Global rules for every phase".
2. Implement the phase's EXACT edits in the files it names. Follow Sol's spec PRECISELY — do NOT invent mechanisms or substitute your own design. If the plan says "one reducer", unify to one; if it says "declarative table", use a table; there is NO rank scheme. 
3. Honor the GLOBAL RULES: generality (NO task-72 / no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject literals in any rule — rules fire by structural/typed signal only), positive-proof only (a gate may reject on absence but ADMIT only on positive evidence), and add the phase's required metamorphic/generality test.
4. Run the phase's ACCEPTANCE TEST exactly as the plan specifies. It MUST exit 0.
5. If it fails, FIX your implementation until the acceptance test passes — do NOT weaken or delete the test to make it pass. If you truly cannot make it pass, stop and report the blocker with the failing output.
6. Do NOT git commit. Do NOT work on other phases. Do NOT disturb outputs/ blobs, the ledger, or launch any long-running/LLM process.

SAFETY (paramount): this code decides whether 3.5M words of new corpus can be cited without fabrication. NEVER make a change that could let a DIFFERENT_WORK or UNRESOLVED_BINDING manifestation be attributed to a claimed source, or let a preprint/working-paper be cited as the journal. When uncertain, quarantine / fail closed. A prior session already hand-made partial "foundation" edits (identity gate in resolve_attribution, from_json re-derivation, evidence_miner ~line 1401 fixed to resolve_attribution(binding,policy), factored derive_binding_core, IDENTITY_PROVEN allowlist) — P1 hardens/verifies these; later phases build on them.

Return the schema: implemented (bool), acceptance_passed (did the test exit 0), the exact test_command, test_output_tail (~last 20 lines), files_changed, a one-paragraph summary, and blocker (empty string if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Binding-gate build plan
2	
3	I reviewed the authoritative design note and the live tree. The six hand edits are directionally correct, but they are not yet sufficient to ship. The remaining critical issues are:
4	
5	- Two independent version derivations still disagree.
6	- Generic call paths still default to `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
7	- Prompt-derived source policy can be lost in fallback contract paths.
8	- Correspondence still trusts the old whole-body `identity.verdict`.
9	- Unresolved/different works still reach the expensive mining lane.
10	- Machine-readable self-metadata is not yet used for positive salvage.
11	- The composer and argument planner still contain live task-specific defaults.
12	
13	The workflow should execute the eight gated phases below in order: P1, P1S, P2–P7. P1S is the explicit source-policy phase required by the operator; numbering P2–P7 remains aligned with the requested phase names.
14	
15	## Global rules for every phase
16	
17	Every phase must preserve these invariants:
18	
19	1. No DOI, title, author, venue, journal, topic, benchmark ID, or subject string may participate in a production decision.
20	2. Decisions may depend only on typed work metadata, artifact structure, self-identifying metadata, byte-derived observations, verified span relationships, and the original prompt.
21	3. The identity gate is always active and is independent of source policy.
22	4. Missing, malformed, stale, or unknown values fail closed.
23	5. `JOURNAL_ONLY` is selected only from a positive constraint in the original prompt. Otherwise use `ANY_VERSION`.
24	6. Each phase must add at least one metamorphic test where identifiers and subject vocabulary are replaced while structure is held constant.
25	7. Do not tune production rules to make the 501-row cohort count come out correctly. Cohort counts are regression observations, not rule inputs.
26	8. Do not advance to the next phase until the phase command exits zero.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## P1 — Finish and harden the six foundation edits
31	
32	### Files
33	
34	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
35	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
36	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
37	- [scripts/cellcog_composer.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py)
38	- [scripts/argument_planner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py)
39	- [scripts/synthesis_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py)
40	- New: `tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py`
41	- New: `tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py`
42	
43	### Exact edits
44	
45	#### 1. `derive_binding_core` and `IDENTITY_PROVEN`
46	
47	Keep both additions in `event_ledger.py`.
48	
49	Correct the string comparison:
50	
51	```python
52	if binding is SAME_WORK:
53	```
54	
55	must become:
56	
57	```python
58	if binding == SAME_WORK:
59	```
60	
61	Keep `derive_semantic_binding(events)` as a thin wrapper. No other identity reducer may be introduced.
62	
63	All verdict consumers must import or call the same `IDENTITY_PROVEN`; do not reproduce its members in another tuple.
64	
65	#### 2. Identity derivation in `ingest_bytes()`
66	
67	Keep the live `observe_text(text) → derive_binding_core(...)` path and the stored:
68	
69	```python
70	profile["semantic_binding"]
71	profile["semantic_binding_basis"]
72	profile["semantic_binding_derived_by"]
73	```
74	
75	Keep `DIFFERENT_WORK` on the quarantine-expression path. It must:
76	
77	- create no claimed-expression edge;
78	- use `kind="unknown"`;
79	- use an expression ID structurally derived from work ID and disposition;
80	- retain the manifestation bytes.
81	
82	Do not copy `row["semantic_binding"]` into each manifestation.
83	
84	In `migrate()`, compare a row-level cached verdict only to the row’s selected `fulltext_manifestation`, when that relationship is explicitly present. Store the comparison as audit-only data:
85	
86	```python
87	profile["row_binding_cache"] = {
88	    "stored": ...,
89	    "live": ...,
90	    "agrees": bool,
91	}
92	```
93	
94	No resolver or constructor may read this field to grant permission.
95	
96	#### 3. `Attribution` fields
97	
98	Keep:
99	
100	```python
101	identity_verdict
102	disposition
103	reason_code
104	```
105	
106	Define stable constants for dispositions and reason codes in `provenance.py`; do not scatter string literals through callers.
107	
108	Stamp every resolver return, including invalid bindings:
109	
110	- invalid span: `QUARANTINE / SPAN_BINDING_INVALID`;
111	- different work: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK`;
112	- unresolved: `LEAD_ONLY / IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED`;
113	- missing/unknown verdict: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT`;
114	- incomplete: `QUARANTINE / INCOMPLETE_BYTES`;
115	- policy mismatch: `LEAD_ONLY / VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED`;
116	- successful resolution: `ADMIT / ADMITTED`.
117	
118	Every refusal must have:
119	
120	```python
121	admitted is False
122	names_expression_id is None
123	text is None
124	permitted_expression_ids == ()
125	```
126	
127	for identity/integrity failures.
128	
129	#### 4. Resolver identity gate
130	
131	Keep its location after span verification and before completeness and expression policy.
132	
133	Remove the unused local imports of each version constant. Import only the canonical allowlist and the two explicit negative verdicts.
134	
135	Do not compute a permissive fallback from the legacy `profile["identity"]["verdict"]`.
136	
137	#### 5. Strict JSON load
138	
139	Keep live re-derivation and refusal on disagreement, but tighten it:
140	
141	- Missing stored `semantic_binding` is an integrity error, not an old-file compatibility path.
142	- Unknown stored verdict is an integrity error.
143	- Re-derive both verdict and basis.
144	- Use the live verdict and live basis after validation.
145	- A stored row-cache verdict is never used.
146	- Update `_take()` to recognize dataclass defaults mechanically with `dataclasses.MISSING`; do not maintain a special handwritten list such as `{"profile", "kind"}`.
147	
148	Migration is the compatibility path for legacy corpus rows. Strict graph load must not silently migrate an old graph.
149	
150	#### 6. Miner binding call
151	
152	Keep the corrected call:
153	
154	```python
155	graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)
156	```
157	
158	Add a regression proving that exact-span correspondence is visible only when the binding, rather than the bare manifestation ID, is passed.
159	
160	#### Remove live task-specific runtime defaults
161	
162	The current downstream runtime still contains task-specific production values.
163	
164	In `cellcog_composer.py`:
165	
166	- Delete the fixed AI/labor `WRITE_PROMPT` subject.
167	- Delete the fixed `OUTLINE`.
168	- Delete the fixed report title.
169	- Change writer input to accept the compiled `research_contract.Contract`, its question/title, and `research_contract.derive_outline(...)`.
170	- Render source-method prose from `Contract.source_policy.compliance_prose()`.
171	- Card instructions must say “permitted source” or the derived policy description, not always “peer-reviewed journal article.”
172	- `abstract_nodes()` and `methods_nodes()` must receive the contract and derive their wording from it.
173	
174	In `argument_planner.py`:
175	
176	- Remove the production `default_contract()` containing AI/labor facets.
177	- Replace it with `contract_from_research_contract(contract, outline)`.
178	- A no-contract call produces an empty, facet-agnostic contract, not an AI contract.
179	- Any AI fixture belongs under tests or an explicitly named demo helper used only by `__main__`.
180	
181	In `synthesis_contract.py`:
182	
183	- The historical task-specific members have already been removed from `SAFE_CAPS`; preserve that.
184	- Delete the `SAFE_CAPS` authority entirely from production validation.
185	- Capitalized names are permitted only when present in a premise, in the original question/compiled contract, sentence-initial by orthography, or emitted from a typed renderer enum.
186	- Replace fixed `LEVEL_CUES` as an authority with per-contract facet matchers or span receipts. Fixed social-science vocabulary must not decide clinical, legal, or CS evidence.
187	- `argument_planner.owned_is_safe()` must use the same structural predicate, not import a second allowlist.
188	
189	Historical examples may remain in comments and tests, but no runtime default may contain or select the task subject.
190	
191	### Acceptance test
192	
193	Create foundation tests covering:
194	
195	- live foreign DOI derives `DIFFERENT_WORK`;
196	- changing only requested DOI makes the same bytes reject under both policies;
197	- changing row cache from `DIFFERENT_WORK` to `SAME_WORK` does not change live resolution;
198	- missing and unknown verdicts fail closed;
199	- invalid binding is structurally stamped;
200	- JSON verdict tampering is refused;
201	- the corrected `gate_card()` passes its binding.
202	
203	Generality test:
204	
205	- compile and render one clinical, one legal, and one CS prompt;
206	- none may inherit AI, labor-market, journal-only, or 4IR wording;
207	- a capitalized entity absent from premises/contract is rejected;
208	- replacing it with a completely different entity produces the same structural result;
209	- putting that entity into a premise makes it admissible without adding it to an allowlist.
210	
211	Gate:
212	
213	```bash
214	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
215	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q \
216	  tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py \
217	  tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py
218	PYTHONPATH=scripts python scripts/provenance.py
219	```
220	
221	### Depends on
222	
223	None.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## P1S — Infer the adjustable source policy from the original prompt
228	
229	### Files
230	
231	- [scripts/research_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/research_contract.py)
232	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
233	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
234	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
235	- New: `tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py`
236	
237	### Exact prompt-derivation rule
238	
239	Add a deterministic, clause-scoped function in `research_contract.py`:
240	
241	```python
242	derive_version_scope(question: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]
243	```
244	
245	It returns `("JOURNAL_ONLY", evidence_clauses)` only when a clause positively directs the system to use published, peer-reviewed, or journal sources.
246	
247	A positive directive requires both:
248	
249	1. A source-class phrase:
250	
251	```text
252	journal article/source/publication/literature
253	peer-reviewed article/source/publication/literature
254	published article/study/source/paper
255	```
256	
257	2. A directive or exclusivity relation in the same clause:
258	
259	```text
260	use, cite, include, draw on, rely on, restrict to, limit to,
261	must be, should be, ensure sources are, only, exclusively, solely
262	```
263	
264	Negation or widening in the same clause defeats `JOURNAL_ONLY`, including:
265	
266	```text
267	not limited to journals
268	do not restrict to journals
269	include preprints/working papers/accepted manuscripts
270	journal articles and preprints
271	```
272	
273	Mere discussion of journals, publication, peer review, or comparisons between source types does not set the gate.
274	
275	No source-class demand means `ANY_VERSION`.
276	
277	The function must return the exact verbatim clause(s) that justified the restriction. The LLM contract compiler may not invent, remove, or broaden this value.
278	
279	### Wiring
280	
281	In `research_contract.SourcePolicy`, add:
282	
283	```python
284	version_scope: str = "ANY_VERSION"
285	version_scope_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
286	```
287	
288	`_floor_source_policy(question)` sets these exclusively through `derive_version_scope(question)`.
289	
290	In `evidence_miner.load_contract()`:
291	
292	- Every return path—explicit facets, compiled contract, cache, question-term fallback, and no-question fallback—must pass through one finalizer.
293	- The finalizer derives provenance policy from the original `question`, not from the most recently cached contract.
294	- `JOURNAL_ONLY` maps to `provenance.JOURNAL_ONLY`.
295	- `ANY_VERSION` maps to `provenance.ANY_VERSION`.
296	- Remove the current inference from `peer_reviewed_only + excluded_types`.
297	- Remove the `PEER_REVIEWED` intermediate behavior from this two-state version policy.
298	
299	Correct generic defaults:
300	
301	```python
302	Graph.resolve_attribution(..., source_policy=ANY_VERSION)
303	gate_card(...): ... or P.ANY_VERSION
304	MiningContract.source_policy: ANY_VERSION
305	```
306	
307	Keep `journal_attributable()` explicit about passing `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
308	
309	In `mine()`:
310	
311	- The contract-derived policy is authoritative when `question` is nonempty.
312	- An explicit `source_policy` that disagrees with a nonempty prompt-derived policy raises `ValueError`.
313	- Explicit policy remains allowed for low-level tests with `question == ""`.
314	
315	In `event_ledger.py`:
316	
317	- Replace `journal_articles_only: bool = True` in `derive_eligibility()` and `record_eligibility()`.
318	- Accept an explicit `SourcePolicy`, defaulting to `ANY_VERSION`.
319	- Version eligibility must consult the policy’s permitted expression kinds.
320	- A preprint is not unconditionally a discovery lead under `ANY_VERSION`.
321	- Remove task-number language from function semantics.
322	
323	### Acceptance test
324	
325	The same valid, complete preprint fixture must produce:
326	
327	| Prompt | Policy | Result |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| “Summarize the evidence about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
330	| “Use journal articles and preprints about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
331	| “Explain differences between journals and preprint servers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
332	| “Use only peer-reviewed sources about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
333	| “Cite published journal articles about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
334	| “Do not limit this to journal articles; include working papers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT` |
335	
336	For `JOURNAL_ONLY`, assert:
337	
338	```python
339	admitted is False
340	disposition == "LEAD_ONLY"
341	reason_code == "VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED"
342	names_expression_id is None
343	```
344	
345	Metamorphic test: replace X with unrelated clinical, legal, economics, and CS subjects; policy must not change.
346	
347	Gate:
348	
349	```bash
350	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
351	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py
352	```
353	
354	### Depends on
355	
356	P1.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## P2 — Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs
361	
362	### Design decision
363	
364	Use one version reducer. Do not retain two competing derivations and choose between them later.
365	
366	`version_evidence()` becomes the single reducer that returns both:
367	
368	```python
369	VersionDecision(
370	    semantic_binding,
371	    expression_kind,
372	    evidence_key,
373	    basis,
374	)
375	```
376	
377	`ingest_bytes()` must assign its expression kind from this result. `derive_binding_core()` must use the same result when identity is proven.
378	
379	The resolver still validates the resulting pair, but that is an invariant check against tampering or in-memory corruption—not a second version derivation.
380	
381	### Files
382	
383	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
384	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
385	- New: `tests/test_version_derivation_unification.py`
386	
387	### Exact edits
388	
389	In `event_ledger.py`:
390	
391	- Replace `VERSION_EVIDENCE` triples with typed ordered rules that specify both semantic binding and expression kind.
392	- Split working-paper and preprint observations structurally.
393	- Preserve precedence:
394	
395	```text
396	accepted manuscript
397	working paper
398	preprint
399	published/proceedings version
400	no version evidence
401	```
402	
403	- Cover-sheet and article-front voices remain separately recorded.
404	- Accepted/preprint/working evidence vetoes published evidence.
405	- Repository names may be registry data, but no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject may appear in the reducer.
406	- For non-scholarly typed works, derive `official_text` or `registry_record` from `Work.kind`, not scholarly furniture.
407	
408	In `provenance.py`:
409	
410	- `profile()` may retain artifact diagnostics, but it must not independently decide the expression kind.
411	- Make `derive_expression_kind()` a compatibility wrapper over the shared observations and `version_evidence()`, or remove it after all callers migrate.
412	- In `ingest_bytes()`, obtain `VersionDecision` once and use its `expression_kind` and basis.
413	- Store the decision’s evidence key in the profile.
414	- In `Graph.from_json()`, re-run the same reducer and refuse disagreement with:
415	  - stored expression kind;
416	  - the manifestation’s expression node kind;
417	  - stored semantic version when identity is proven.
418	- In `resolve_attribution()`, after identity and before completeness, validate the pair against one declarative compatibility table.
419	- Any incompatible pair returns:
420	
421	```python
422	admitted=False
423	disposition="QUARANTINE"
424	reason_code="DERIVATION_CONFLICT"
425	names_expression_id=None
426	text=None
427	permitted_expression_ids=()
428	```
429	
430	### Exact pair decision table
431	
432	Assume valid span, identity-proven bytes, and completeness. Correspondence exceptions apply only to the exact bound span.
433	
434	| Semantic binding | Own expression kind | Pair status | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `ANY_VERSION` |
435	|---|---|---|---|---|
436	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `journal_version` | consistent | ADMIT journal | ADMIT journal |
437	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `proceedings_version` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT proceedings |
438	| `VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED` | `accepted_manuscript` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT accepted manuscript |
439	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `working_paper` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT working paper |
440	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `preprint` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT preprint |
441	| `SAME_WORK` | `official_text` for typed case/statute | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT official text |
442	| `SAME_WORK` | `registry_record` for typed trial/registry work | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT registry record |
443	| `SAME_WORK` | `unknown` | unresolved version, not conflict | LEAD_ONLY | LEAD_ONLY |
444	| Any proven semantic version | Any other own expression kind | conflict | QUARANTINE | QUARANTINE |
445	
446	In particular:
447	
448	```text
449	VERSION_OF_PREPRINT + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
450	VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
451	VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED + working_paper/preprint/accepted_manuscript = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
452	```
453	
454	There is no rank scheme and no “choose the safer-looking label.”
455	
456	### Metamorphic acceptance vector
457	
458	Create one accepted fixture, then mutate only structural version furniture:
459	
460	1. Published furniture → `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED / journal_version`.
461	2. Add accepted-manuscript stamp → both outputs change to accepted.
462	3. Replace accepted stamp with working-paper stamp → both outputs change to preprint/working.
463	4. Relabel the graph expression node to `journal_version` without changing bytes → loader refuses and resolver returns `DERIVATION_CONFLICT`.
464	5. Repeat with unrelated clinical, legal, and CS titles/authors; results remain identical.
465	
466	The real seven known conflicts must become zero admissible conflicts without listing their identifiers.
467

631	### Exact data model
632	
633	Add an explicit `IdentityReceipt` dataclass containing at least:
634	
635	```python

655	For field-based PDF metadata, offsets are offsets inside the named metadata field. For XMP/HTML/JATS, offsets are byte or decoded-character offsets in the raw artifact. The location type must make the coordinate space explicit.
656	
657	Extend `Manifestation` with optional raw-artifact lineage:

667	
668	`migrate()` matches the selected `fulltext_manifestation` or exact `text_sha256` against `row["manifestations"]` and passes its raw blob reference. It must not copy a receipt or verdict from the row.
669	
670	### Exact extraction rule
671	
672	Run `reresolve_unresolved_metadata(graph, blob_store)` only after baseline manifestations exist, and only over live `UNRESOLVED_BINDING`.
673	
674	Admissible self-fields are:
675	
676	#### PDF, through PyMuPDF/fitz
677	
678	- DOI: PDF Info or XMP self-metadata fields.
679	- Title: PDF Info/XMP title.
680	- Authors: PDF Info/XMP author/creator.
681	
682	Do not inspect rendered body or reference text.
683	
684	#### HTML
685	
686	Inside `<head>` only:
687	
688	- identifier: `citation_doi`, `DC.identifier`;
689	- title: `citation_title`, `DC.title`;
690	- author: repeated `citation_author`, `DC.creator`.
691	
692	#### JATS
693	
694	Inside `<front><article-meta>` only:
695	
696	- identifier: `<article-id pub-id-type="doi">`;
697	- title: `<article-title>`;
698	- authors: `<contrib contrib-type="author">`, using structured name fields.
699	
700	Never search `<body>` or `<back>`.
701	
702	### Positive promotion rule
703	
704	Normalize DOI by parsing DOI URI/prefix forms, case-folding, percent-decoding, and stripping terminal citation punctuation.
705	
706	Normalize title with Unicode NFKC, entity decoding, case-folding, punctuation-to-space, and whitespace collapse. Title match is exact after normalization—no fuzzy threshold.
707	
708	Normalize authors structurally:
709	
710	- compare requested family name to a structured surname field; or
711	- compare an exact normalized full-name sequence;
712	- a single requested surname must equal a metadata surname token, not be a substring.
713	
714	Promote only when one of these holds:
715	
716	1. Exact requested DOI in a permitted self-identifier field.
717	2. Exact normalized requested title and at least one requested author in permitted self-identity fields.
718	
719	Conflict rule:
720	
721	- if self-identifier fields contain both target and foreign DOI values, remain unresolved with `IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT`;
722	- title/author evidence cannot override a conflicting self-identifier;
723	- absence of metadata changes nothing.
724	
725	A verified receipt becomes an observation consumed by `derive_binding_core()`. The reducer then re-derives `SAME_WORK` or the appropriate version verdict. Do not directly assign a promoted verdict in the salvage loop.
726	
727	### Loader revalidation
728	
729	`Graph.from_json(..., blob_store=...)` must:
730	
731	1. load raw bytes by immutable blob ID;
732	2. verify raw hash;
733	3. rerun the named metadata extractor/version;
734	4. re-find the exact raw match at the recorded location;
735	5. re-normalize the value;
736	6. re-evaluate it against the Work;
737	7. compare the canonical live receipt to the stored receipt;
738	8. re-run `derive_binding_core()` using verified receipts;
739	9. refuse the graph on any mismatch or unavailable receipt artifact.
740	
741	A graph with receipts cannot load without access to the referenced blob store.
742	
743	### OCR status
744	
745	Do not implement OCR.
746	
747	Document in `docs/identity_metadata_salvage.md`:

751	reason: no installed/revalidatable OCR backend; tesseract unavailable and no sudo
752	effect: residual glyph-header manifestations remain LEAD_ONLY
753	```
754	
755	An `OCR`-typed receipt supplied through JSON must be refused as an unsupported receipt type.

1336	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}

1338	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'unreadable ({cinfo["reason"]})',
1339	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1340	    if binding == DIFFERENT_WORK:
1341	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'wrong paper ({binfo["reason"]})',
1342	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1343	    # ---- THE VERSION VETO. A PRECONDITION, checked before anything may be admitted. -------------
1344	    if binding in NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE:
1345	        return DISCOVERY_LEAD, {
1346	            'reason': NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE[binding]
1347	                      + (' and the task demands journal articles only' if journal_articles_only else ''),
1348	            'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1349	    if binding == UNRESOLVED:
1350	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'we cannot show this text is the work we cite',
1351	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1352	    if cls == C_CITATION:
1353	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'no content', 'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1354	
1355	    # ---- THE POSTCONDITION. It does not care which branch answered, or in what order. -----------
1356	    assert binding not in NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE, (
1357	        f'ADMISSIBLE was about to be minted for a {binding} binding. THE V9 P0 IS REOPENED.')
1358	    return ADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'{binding.lower().replace("_", " ")}; {cinfo["reason"]}',
1359	                        'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1360	
1361	
1362	# ---- 6. weight components — "high_quality" IS FORBIDDEN ---------------------------------------
1363	
1364	@dataclass(frozen=True)
1365	class WeightComponents:
1366	    """Sol: "high_quality" must render as its COMPONENTS, each with provenance.
1367	
1368	    There is deliberately NO `.label` and NO `__str__` that collapses to a scalar. A single word
1369	    ("high quality") is exactly the kind of claim that outruns its evidence — one strong component
1370	    carries the two weak ones, and nobody can see it happen.
1371	    """
1372	    components: dict[str, Any]
1373	    provenance: dict[str, str]
1374	
1375	    def render(self) -> str:
1376	        return ', '.join(f'{k}={v} [{self.provenance.get(k, "?")}]'
1377	                         for k, v in self.components.items())
1378

1272	                            source_policy: SourcePolicy = JOURNAL_ONLY) -> Attribution:
1273	        """MAY THIS BOUND SPAN be attributed under THIS task's instruction, and TO WHAT ID?
1274	
1275	        Sol V9 §4 moved this API from manifestation-wide permission to BINDING-SPECIFIC permission:
1276	
1277	            resolve_attribution(binding, attribution_policy)

1279	        `ref` is therefore a BINDING (the dict `bind_span()` returns, which is also what a card carries).
1280	        A bare `manifestation_id` string is still accepted — the honest whole-document question, asked by
1281	        the census and by the quarantine sweep — and it answers WITHOUT any per-span widening: a
1282	        manifestation-wide question can only be answered with manifestation-wide evidence, i.e. the
1283	        document's own expression plus a whole-document `exact_copy_of`. A SpanCorrespondence grants ONE
1284	        SPAN and therefore cannot answer a question that was not asked about a span.
1285	
1286	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
1287	          1. the bytes are usable as evidence at all — complete, uncorrupted, the right work; AND
1288	          2. some expression this span MAY LEGALLY NAME is of a kind THIS POLICY PERMITS.
1289

1291	        verbatim span said "633 per cent" because the PDF was a Caesar-shifted encoding of "300".

1803:def migrate(corpus: list[dict]) -> Graph:

1438	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
1439	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
1440	
1441	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us
1442	        already. `to_json()` writes the text, its hash, its word count and its derived profile, and any
1443	        copy of that file — hand-edited, half-written, merged, or produced by an older version of this
1444	        module — can disagree with itself in every one of those places. A loader that accepts what it
1445	        is handed re-opens the hole FROM THE OTHER SIDE: the enforcement point would read a
1446	        `complete: true` that no bytes support, and would enforce it.
1447	
1448	        REFUSES, NEVER REPAIRS:
1449	          - a content_hash that is not sha256(text)                  [the span gate's only anchor]
1450	          - an n_words that is not len(text.split())                 [the count that describes a
1451	                                                                       document we no longer hold —
1452	                                                                       the exact defect event_ledger
1453	                                                                       catches in the live corpus]
1454	          - a stored extractability verdict the text does not support
1455	          - a `complete: true` (or false) THE REGISTRY DOES NOT DERIVE for that kind and that text
1456	          - a manifestation naming an expression or a work that does not exist
1457	          - a manifestation whose expression belongs to a DIFFERENT work
1458	          - an expression naming a work that does not exist
1459	          - an edge whose endpoints do not exist, or that carries no basis
1460	          - an ASSERTED span-preserving edge whose basis does not AUTHENTICATE span preservation
1461	          - any unknown field, anywhere (a schema drift silently drops the field a gate reads)

1471	        def _take(cl, d: dict, what: str):

1475	                           f'this schema, and a field a gate reads may be missing from it')

1500	
1501	        for d in data.get('manifestations', []):
1502	            m = _take(Manifestation, d, f"manifestation {d.get('id')!r}")
1503	            if m is None:
1504	                continue

1509	            real = hashlib.sha256((m.text or '').encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()

1512	                           f'({real[:12]}…) — every span bound to it is bound to nothing')
1513	                continue

1515	            # 2. references resolve, and resolve TO THE SAME WORK.

1519	                err.append(f'{where}: is filed under work {m.work_id!r} but its expression '
1520	                           f'{m.expression_id!r} belongs to work '
1521	                           f'{g.expressions[m.expression_id].work_id!r}')
1522	            if m.work_id not in g.works:

1526	            words = len((m.text or '').split())

1803	def migrate(corpus: list[dict]) -> Graph:
1804	    g = Graph()
1805	    for row in corpus:
1806	        work, claimed_id, claimed_kind = ensure_work(
1807	            g, doi=row.get('doi') or '', title=row.get('title') or '',
1808	            authors=list(row.get('authors') or []), year=row.get('year'),
1809	            venue=row.get('venue') or '', source_type=str(row.get('type') or ''))
1810	
1811	        # ---- the bytes we hold, if any ----------------------------------------------------------
1812	        for fieldname in ('fulltext', 'abstract'):
1813	            text = (row.get(fieldname) or '').strip()
1814	            if not text:
1815	                continue
1816	            # The row's abstract came from the BIBLIOGRAPHY, independently of whatever body we
1817	            # fetched — which is exactly what makes it usable as a probe against that body. It is
1818	            # never used to probe itself.
1819	            indep = (row.get('abstract') or '') if fieldname == 'fulltext' else ''
1820	
1821	            # The fetcher's own label is EVIDENCE, NOT TRUTH. `fulltext_source='working_paper'`
1822	            # records WHICH FETCHER RAN, not what the artifact is: it is false on 5 of the 6 rows
1823	            # that carry it. The bytes decide; the label is recorded and contradicted in the open.
1824	            claimed_src = row.get('fulltext_source') or 'publisher_or_oa'
1825	
1826	            # THE LOCATOR. wp_fetch.py NEVER RECORDED THE URL IT FETCHED FROM, so for the rows it
1827	            # touched the only URL on the row is `oa_url` -- left by an EARLIER fetcher and pointing
1828	            # at the PUBLISHER (aeaweb.org!). A locator that names the journal while the bytes are a
1829	            # working paper is worse than no locator, so it is not silently adopted.
1830	            locator, lstatus = row.get('oa_url') or None, 'RECORDED'
1831	            if not locator:
1832	                lstatus = 'NOT_RECORDED_BY_FETCHER'
1833	            elif claimed_src == 'working_paper':
1834	                lstatus = 'CONTRADICTS_CONTENT (oa_url points at the publisher; these bytes were ' \
1835	                          'fetched by wp_fetch, which never recorded its own URL)'
1836	
1837	            ingest_bytes(g, work, text, text_field=fieldname, fetched_by=claimed_src,
1838	                         locator=locator, locator_status=lstatus, claimed_id=claimed_id,
1839	                         claimed_kind=claimed_kind, independent_abstract=indep)
1840	    return g
1841	
1842	
1843	# =================================================================================================
1844	# 5. THE HONEST CENSUS
1845	# =================================================================================================
1846	
1847	def census(g: Graph, corpus: list[dict]) -> None:
1848	    P = print
1849	    P('=' * 100)
1850	    P('PROVENANCE CENSUS — every label re-derived from the bytes it describes')
1851	    P('=' * 100)
1852	
1853	    P(f'\n  WORKS                 {len(g.works)}')
1854	    P(f'  EXPRESSIONS           {len(g.expressions)}')
1855	    P(f'  MANIFESTATIONS        {len(g.manifestations)}   (bytes we actually hold)')
1856	    P(f'  EDGES                 {len(g.edges)}')
1857	
1858	    P('\n--- MANIFESTATIONS BY EXPRESSION KIND (what version the BYTES say they are) ' + '-' * 24)
1859	    byk: dict[str, list[Manifestation]] = {}
1860	    for m in g.manifestations.values():
1861	        byk.setdefault(g.expressions[m.expression_id].kind, []).append(m)
1862	    for k in EXPRESSION_KINDS:
1863	        ms = byk.get(k, [])
1864	        if ms:
1865	            P(f'  {k:<22} {len(ms):>3}   ({sum(x.n_words for x in ms):>7,} words held)')
1866	
1867	    P('\n--- MANIFESTATIONS BY CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT KIND ' + '-' * 47)
1868	    bya: dict[str, list[Manifestation]] = {}
1869	    for m in g.manifestations.values():
1870	        bya.setdefault(m.profile['artifact_kind'], []).append(m)
1871	    for k in ARTIFACT_KINDS:
1872	        ms = bya.get(k, [])
1873	        if ms:
1874	            ok = sum(1 for x in ms if x.profile['complete'])
1875	            P(f'  {k:<22} {len(ms):>3}   complete: {ok:>2}   not complete: {len(ms) - ok:>2}')
1876	
1877	    # ---- THE NUMBER THE ANSWER ACTUALLY RESTS ON --------------------------------------------
1878	    P('\n--- ADMISSIBILITY FOR A JOURNAL-ONLY ANSWER (task 72) ' + '-' * 45)
1879	    jok = [m for m in g.manifestations.values() if g.journal_attributable(m.id)]
1880	    wponly, unres, dead = [], [], []
1881	    for m in g.manifestations.values():
1882	        if g.journal_attributable(m.id):
1883	            continue
1884	        k = g.expressions[m.expression_id].kind
1885	        if k in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
1886	            wponly.append(m)
1887	        # Read from the registry, not from a literal list kept beside it. The literal list is how a
1888	        # new kind gets silently counted as something else: `citation_only` was born and 13 citation
1889	        # stubs would have been reported as "unresolved versions" — a claim about VERSIONS, made about
1890	        # documents we do not have.
1891	        elif not m.profile['complete'] and m.profile['artifact_kind'] in NOT_A_DOCUMENT_KINDS:
1892	            dead.append(m)
1893	        else:
1894	            unres.append(m)
1895	
1896	    P(f'  JOURNAL-ATTRIBUTABLE            {len(jok):>3}   spans from these may be printed as journal findings')
1897	    P(f'  WORKING-PAPER / PREPRINT ONLY   {len(wponly):>3}   INELIGIBLE — citing them breaks the journal-only')
1898	    P(f'  {"":<32}      instruction; citing the journal with THEIR text is fabrication')
1899	    P(f'  UNRESOLVED VERSION              {len(unres):>3}   bytes carry no version furniture — cannot be claimed')
1900	    P(f'  NOT A DOCUMENT                  {len(dead):>3}   landing pages, corrupt extractions, wrong work')
1901	
1902	    claimed_ft = sum(1 for c in corpus if c.get('content_status') == 'FULLTEXT')
1903	    P(f'\n  THE CORPUS CLAIMS               {claimed_ft:>3}   rows with content_status=FULLTEXT')
1904	    P(f'  THE BYTES SUPPORT               {len(jok):>3}   journal-attributable manifestations')
1905	    P(f'  {"":>32}   ^ THIS IS WORSE THAN THE CORPUS CLAIMS. IT IS ALSO TRUE.')
1906	
1907	    P('\n--- EDGES (nothing is ASSERTED that the bytes do not prove) ' + '-' * 39)
1908	    for t in EDGE_TYPES:
1909	        es = [e for e in g.edges if e.type == t]
1910	        if es:
1911	            a = sum(1 for e in es if e.status == 'ASSERTED')
1912	            P(f'  {t:<24} {len(es):>3}   ASSERTED: {a}   PROPOSED: {len(es) - a}')
1913	    P('  (0 ASSERTED span-preserving edges: we hold no journal bytes to compare against, so no')
1914	    P('   working paper has EARNED the right to be cited as its journal article.)')
1915	
1916	    # ---- every recovered paper, profiled -----------------------------------------------------
1917	    P('\n--- CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE OF EVERY MANIFESTATION WE HOLD ' + '-' * 31)
1918	    P(f'  {"words":>6} {"kind":<19} {"ver":<12} {"extract":<9} {"ident":<12} {"J?":<3} source')
1919	    for m in sorted(g.manifestations.values(), key=lambda m: (

249	    """The verbatim excerpt of `span` that supports the declared `level`, or '' if the span never states

#: ══ STABLE DISPOSITION / REASON-CODE CONSTANTS (Sol binding-gate §1, edit 3) ═══════════════════════
#: Every resolver return is stamped with one DISPOSITION and one REASON_CODE. Callers switch on these
#: MACHINE TOKENS — never on refusal prose (which is human-facing and may change). Defining them ONCE,
#: here, is the rule: a string literal scattered through a caller is a token nobody can grep for and a
#: typo that fails open. `DISPOSITIONS` / `REASON_CODES` are the closed registries the tests assert on.
DISPOSITION_ADMIT      = 'ADMIT'
DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY  = 'LEAD_ONLY'
DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE = 'QUARANTINE'
DISPOSITIONS = frozenset({DISPOSITION_ADMIT, DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY, DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE})

RC_ADMITTED                 = 'ADMITTED'
RC_SPAN_BINDING_INVALID     = 'SPAN_BINDING_INVALID'
RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK  = 'IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK'
RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED      = 'IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED'
RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT = 'IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT'
RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES         = 'INCOMPLETE_BYTES'

RC_DERIVATION_CONFLICT      = 'DERIVATION_CONFLICT'

                          RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED, RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT, RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES,
                          RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED, RC_DERIVATION_CONFLICT})

543	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
544	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
545	
546	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
547	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
548	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
549	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
550	    """
551	    words = text.split()
552	    header = text[:header_chars]
553	    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
554	    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
555	    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
556	    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
557	    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
558	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
559	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
560	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
561	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
562	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
563	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
564	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
565	    return {
566	        'word_count': len(words),
567	        'char_count': len(text),
568	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
569	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
570	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
571	        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------
572	        # A stamp means something different depending on WHO PRINTED IT. The article's own front
573	        # matter is the DOCUMENT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT ITSELF; the cover sheet is THE LIBRARY'S OPINION OF
574	        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a
575	        # library's "citation for the published version" was scored as the article calling itself the
576	        # version of record. They are separated now, and `derive_semantic_binding` weighs them by voice.
577	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
578	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
579	        'published_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(cover)),
580	        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
581	        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
582	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
583	        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),
584	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
585	        'header_real_words': header_real,
586	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
587	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
588	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
589	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
590	        'identity_window': header,
591	        'header_text': header[:400],
592	        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
593	        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
594	        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
595	        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
596	        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
597	        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
598	        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
599	        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
600	        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
601	        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
602	        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
603	        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
604	    }
605

608	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
609	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1213	        of those is a span that BINDS CLEANLY AND THEN VERIFIES — its stored text is empty, the empty
1214	        slice still equals it, and the content hash is perfectly correct. The card would name a real
1215	        paper, carry a real hash, pass verify_span(), and quote NOTHING FROM IT. That is a fabricated
1216	        citation with a valid receipt, which is the exact shape of every defect in this file's header.
1217	        A negative offset is worse than empty: `text[-5:]` silently indexes from the END and binds a
1218	        span from somewhere else entirely.
1219	
1220	        So they are REFUSED, not clamped. Clamping would invent a span the miner never mined.
1221	
1222	        RETURNS IDENTIFIERS, NOT PROSE. `may_name` used to hand back English SENTENCES, so a consumer
1223	        deciding what was legal had to string-match a display string. Prose is a cache; an id resolves.
1224	        """
1225	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
1226	        if m is None:
1227	            raise SpanBindingError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r} — a span cannot bind to '
1228	                                   f'bytes we do not hold')
1229	        for nm, v in (('start', start), ('end', end)):
1230	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
1231	                raise SpanBindingError(f'{nm} offset must be an int, got {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')
1232	        n = len(m.text)
1233	        if start < 0 or end < 0:
1234	            raise SpanBindingError(
1235	                f'NEGATIVE span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — Python would index from the '
1236	                f'END of the text and bind a span from a different part of the document')
1237	        if end <= start:
1238	            raise SpanBindingError(
1239	                f'EMPTY OR REVERSED span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — it slices to "" and '
1240	                f'would then verify perfectly against nothing, under a real citation')
1241	        if end > n:
1242	            raise SpanBindingError(
1243	                f'span [{start}:{end}] runs past the end of {manifestation_id} ({n} chars) — Python '
1244	                f'would truncate it silently, and the stored text would not be the span that was mined')
1245	        b = dict(
1246	            manifestation_id=manifestation_id,
1247	            content_hash=m.content_hash,
1248	            span_start=start, span_end=end,
1249	            text=m.text[start:end],
1250	            expression_id=m.expression_id,
1251	        )

def _slug(s: str, n: int = 40) -> str:
    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', (s or '').lower()).strip('-')[:n] or 'x'


def derive_source_type(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
    """WHAT KIND OF SOURCE DOES THIS ROW DECLARE ITSELF TO BE?
    -> (work_kind, the expression the metadata CLAIMS exists, basis)

    `migrate()` used to open with `jid = f'{wid}:journal_version'` for every row it was ever handed.
    Sol: "That is not valid for a judicial opinion or statute." It is not even valid for a PREPRINT
    row — a posted-content record claims no journal version at all, and minting one creates, out of
    nothing, a node whose whole meaning is "a peer-reviewed article exists here", for a work that may
    never have been through peer review.

a=g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION); print('  ANY disp', a.disposition, a.reason_code, a.admitted, a.names_expression_id)
aj=g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.JOURNAL_ONLY); print('  JRN disp', aj.disposition, aj.reason_code, aj.admitted)

print('FOREIGN:', m2.profile['semantic_binding'])

# furniture, profiled as `unknown`, and every card built on them was refused as
            # INADMISSIBLE. The tests would have "passed" for a reason that had nothing to do with
            # what they were testing. So it refuses to hand back a document it could not build.
            raise AssertionError(f'journal_doc({doi!r}) did not build a complete journal article: '
                                 f'{pr["artifact_kind"]} {pr["incomplete_because"]}')
        v, chs = chunk_document('d', g.manifestations[mid].text)
        p = {'doi': doi, 'authors': ['Author'], 'year': 2020, 'venue': 'Some Journal', 'title': title,
             'abstract': '', 'manifestation_id': mid, '_source_version': 'x', '_text_field': 'fulltext'}
        return g, v, chs, p

    good = {'span': 'We find that one more robot per thousand workers reduces the employment-to-population '
                    'ratio by 0.2 percentage points (standard error 0.05).',
            'effect': '0.2', 'unit': 'percentage points', 'comparator': 'per thousand workers',
            'outcome': 'employment-to-population ratio', 'population': 'commuting zones',
            'geography': 'United States', 'period': '1990 and 2007', 'technology': 'robot',
            'industry': '', 'unit_of_analysis': 'region', 'design': 'quasi-experimental',
            'uncertainty': 'standard error 0.05', 'horizon': 'long-run', 'mechanisms': []}
    r = new_rejects()
    c = gate_card(dict(good), view, ch, paper, pw, MC, r)
    ck('a TRUE, complete tuple is ADMITTED', c is not None and c['complete_tuple'],
       f'rejects={ {k:v for k,v in r.items() if v} }')
    if c:
        ck('the stored span is a SLICE OF THE SOURCE, not the model\'s string',
           view.src[c['span_start']:c['span_end']].strip().startswith('We find that one more robot'),
           repr(view.src[c['span_start']:c['span_end']])[:90])
        ck('claim is DERIVED after the gate and carries no figure absent from the span',
           not (number_tokens(c['claim']) - set(c['span_numbers'])), c['claim'])

    # --- 60 REAL CHARS + AN INVENTED TAIL
    r = new_rejects()
    tail = dict(good, span='We find that one more robot per thousand workers reduces employment by 47 '
                           'percent across every industry in the economy.', effect='47', unit='percent')
    ck('60 real chars followed by an INVENTED TAIL is REJECTED',
       gate_card(tail, view, ch, paper, pw, MC, r) is None, 'the invented tail was admitted')

    # --- A FABRICATED FIGURE IN A FIELD
    r = new_rejects()
    fab = dict(good, effect='0.39')          # 0.39 is in the TABLE, not in this span
    ck('a figure not in THIS span is REJECTED even though it is elsewhere in the paper',
       gate_card(fab, view, ch, paper, pw, MC, r) is None and r['number_not_in_span'] == 1)

    # --- THE SUBSTRING ATTACK, END TO END
    r = new_rejects()
    sub = {'span': 'Robots per thousand workers   -0.39   (0.08)', 'effect': '0.3',
           'unit': 'percentage points', 'outcome': 'employment', 'unit_of_analysis': 'region',
           'design': 'observational', 'uncertainty': '(0.08)', 'population': '', 'geography': '',
           'period': '', 'technology': 'robots', 'industry': '', 'comparator': 'per thousand workers'}
    tb = [x for x in chunks if x.section == 'tables']
    if tb:
        ck('a fabricated 0.3 does NOT pass because the span contains -0.39',
           gate_card(sub, view, tb[0], paper, pw, MC, r) is None and r['number_not_in_span'] == 1)
        r = new_rejects()
        row = dict(sub, effect='-0.39')
        got = gate_card(row, view, tb[0], paper, pw, MC, r)
        ck('a TABLE ROW is minable (the caption/header supplies the outcome; provenance recorded)',
           got is not None, f'rejects={ {k:v for k,v in r.items() if v} }')
        if got:
            ck('the table card records that `outcome` came from the CHUNK, not the span',
               got['field_provenance'].get('outcome') in ('chunk', 'span'), str(got['field_provenance']))
    else:
        ck('table block detected', False, 'no table chunk was produced')

    # --- ELLIPSIS = NOT WHOLE
    r = new_rejects()
    ell = dict(good, span='We find that one more robot per thousand workers ... by 0.2 percentage points.')
    ck('an ELIDED span ("<real> ... <invented>") is REJECTED',
       gate_card(ell, view, ch, paper, pw, MC, r) is None)

    # --- ORPHAN NUMBER. The old GATE 6 (`not (unit or comparator) and not outcome -> reject`) is now
    #     the registry's rule for `quantitative_estimate`: required_any = [[unit, comparator, outcome]].
    #     SAME REJECTION, SAME CARDS, and it is now a data edit rather than an `if`. D1 does not move.

input.content:
"""P1 — foundation acceptance battery for the binding gate.

Every vector runs through the REAL chain: migrate() / ingest_bytes -> the ONE identity reducer
(event_ledger.derive_binding_core) -> Graph.resolve_attribution(binding|mid, policy), and where the
disk is the attack surface, through Graph.to_json / Graph.from_json. No Attribution is mocked and no
successful verdict is hand-assigned; the tests only ever READ the structured identity layer
(identity_verdict / disposition / reason_code) the resolver stamps.

Generality (Sol global rule 1 & 6): no rule under test may key on a DOI, title, author, venue, or
subject literal. The metamorphic tests replace every identifier and subject term while holding the
STRUCTURE constant, and assert the structural verdict is unchanged.
"""
import copy

import pytest

import provenance as P
from provenance import (DISPOSITION_ADMIT, DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY, DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE,
                        RC_ADMITTED, RC_SPAN_BINDING_INVALID, RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK,
                        RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED, RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT, RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES,
                        RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED)

FILLER = ('This paper studies the question at length across many pages of careful analysis, '
          'reporting the design, the data and the estimates in full. ') * 110


def _row(requested_doi, body_doi, *, title='A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
         authors=('Adams', 'Brown'), byline='By Alice Adams and Bob Brown', venue='Journal of Widgets',
         results='We find the effect is 0.2 units (standard error 0.05) across 722 sites.', **extra):
    """A single corpus row whose FULLTEXT carries `body_doi` in its own front matter. The row asks for
    `requested_doi`. When the two agree the bytes are SAME_WORK; when they differ the front matter is a
    foreign DOI and the bytes are a stranger's paper (DIFFERENT_WORK) — positive evidence, both ways."""
    src = (f'{title}\ndoi: {body_doi}\n{byline}\n'
           f'1. Introduction\n{FILLER}\n4. Results\n{results}\n')
    row = {'doi': requested_doi, 'title': title, 'authors': list(authors),
           'venue': venue, 'year': 2020, 'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': src, 'abstract': ''}
    row.update(extra)
    return row


def _one(rows):
    g = P.migrate(rows)
    m = next(iter(g.manifestations.values()))
    return g, m


# ── identity: the positive-proof core ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_live_foreign_doi_derives_different_work():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.identity_verdict == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.names_expression_id is None
    assert att.text is None
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


def test_matching_doi_is_same_work_and_admits_under_any_version():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'SAME_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.admitted is True
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_ADMIT
    assert att.reason_code == RC_ADMITTED
    assert att.names_expression_id is not None


def test_changing_only_requested_doi_rejects_the_same_bytes_under_both_policies():
    body = '10.2222/bbbb'
    # Same bytes, two requested identities. Only the requested DOI differs.
    _, ok = _one([_row(body, body)])
    _, foreign = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', body)])
    assert ok.text == foreign.text  # the bytes are byte-identical
    for policy in (P.JOURNAL_ONLY, P.ANY_VERSION):
        g_ok, m_ok = _one([_row(body, body)])
        g_f, m_f = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', body)])
        assert g_f.resolve_attribution(m_f.id, policy).admitted is False
        # the ONLY thing that changed was the requested DOI; the admissible one still resolves
        assert g_ok.resolve_attribution(m_ok.id, P.ANY_VERSION).admitted is True


def test_row_binding_cache_is_audit_only_and_cannot_grant_permission():
    # The row LIES: it caches SAME_WORK, but the bytes self-identify as a different work.
    g, m = _one([_row('10.9999/zzzz', '10.2222/bbbb', semantic_binding='SAME_WORK')])
    cache = m.profile.get('row_binding_cache')
    assert cache == {'stored': 'SAME_WORK', 'live': 'DIFFERENT_WORK', 'agrees': False}
    # Live resolution ignores the cache entirely and still quarantines.
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
    assert att.admitted is False


def test_row_cache_lying_different_work_does_not_suppress_a_true_same_work():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb', semantic_binding='DIFFERENT_WORK')])
    assert m.profile['row_binding_cache']['stored'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'SAME_WORK'
    assert g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION).admitted is True


# ── fail-closed on missing / unknown verdicts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_missing_verdict_fails_closed():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    m.profile['semantic_binding'] = None
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


def test_unknown_verdict_fails_closed():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    m.profile['semantic_binding'] = 'SAMEISH_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


def test_unresolved_binding_is_lead_only_not_quarantine():
    # A readable header, a generic 2-word title, no requested author present, no DOI tie: UNRESOLVED.
    row = _row('', '', title='Widget Study', authors=(), byline='An untitled report',
               venue='Journal of Widgets')
    g, m = _one([row])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED
    assert att.admitted is False


# ── structural stamping of an invalid binding ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_invalid_binding_is_structurally_stamped():
    g, m = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    binding = g.bind_span(m.id, 5, 60)
    tampered = dict(binding)
    tampered['content_hash'] = 'deadbeef' * 8          # no longer the manifestation's hash
    att = g.resolve_attribution(tampered, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_SPAN_BINDING_INVALID
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.names_expression_id is None
    assert att.text is None
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


def test_incomplete_bytes_are_stamped_and_name_nothing():
    # A stub: readable, identity-proven by its own DOI, but far too short to carry a finding.
    src = 'A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism\ndoi: 10.2222/bbbb\nBy Alice Adams and Bob Brown\n'
    row = {'doi': '10.2222/bbbb', 'title': 'A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
           'authors': ['Adams', 'Brown'], 'venue': 'Journal of Widgets', 'year': 2020,
           'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': src, 'abstract': ''}
    g, m = _one([row])
    if m.profile['complete']:
        pytest.skip('fixture is unexpectedly complete')
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.JOURNAL_ONLY)
    assert att.disposition == DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE
    assert att.reason_code == RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES
    assert att.admitted is False
    assert att.permitted_expression_ids == ()


# ── JSON tampering is refused, never repaired ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_clean_graph_round_trips():
    g, _ = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    reloaded = P.Graph.from_json(g.to_json())
    assert len(reloaded.manifestations) == len(g.manifestations)


def test_json_verdict_tampering_is_refused():
    g, _ = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    d = g.to_json()
    for mm in d['manifestations']:
        mm['profile']['semantic_binding'] = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'   # bytes re-derive SAME_WORK
    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
        P.Graph.from_json(d)


def test_json_missing_verdict_is_an_integrity_error():
    g, _ = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    d = g.to_json()
    for mm in d['manifestations']:
        mm['profile'].pop('semantic_binding', None)
    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
        P.Graph.from_json(d)


def test_json_unknown_verdict_is_an_integrity_error():
    g, _ = _one([_row('10.2222/bbbb', '10.2222/bbbb')])
    d = g.to_json()
    for mm in d['manifestations']:
        mm['profile']['semantic_binding'] = 'WAT'
    with pytest.raises(P.GraphIntegrityError):
        P.Graph.from_json(d)


# ── the corrected gate_card() passes its BINDING, not a bare id ──────────────────────────────────

def test_gate_card_passes_the_binding_not_the_bare_manifestation_id(monkeypatch):
    import evidence_miner as EM
    src = ('A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism\ndoi: 10.2222/bbbb\n'
           'By Alice Adams and Bob Brown\n1. Introduction\n' + FILLER +
           '\n4. Results\nWe find that the widget mechanism reduces the failure rate by 0.2 '
           'percentage points (standard error 0.05) across 722 independent sites.\n')
    row = {'doi': '10.2222/bbbb', 'title': 'A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
           'authors': ['Adams', 'Brown'], 'venue': 'Journal of Widgets', 'year': 2020,
           'type': 'journal-article', 'fulltext': src, 'abstract': ''}
    g = P.migrate([row])
    mid = next(iter(g.manifestations))
    view, chunks = EM.chunk_document('d', g.manifestations[mid].text)
    chunk = next(c for c in chunks if 'failure rate' in c.text)
    paper = {'doi': '10.2222/bbbb', 'authors': ['Adams', 'Brown'], 'year': 2020,
             'venue': 'Journal of Widgets', 'title': 'A Rigorous Study of the Widget Mechanism',
             'abstract': '', 'manifestation_id': mid, '_source_version': 'x', '_text_field': 'fulltext'}
    pw = EM.paper_window(view, chunks, paper)
    raw = {'span': 'We find that the widget mechanism reduces the failure rate by 0.2 percentage '
                   'points (standard error 0.05) across 722 independent sites.',
           'effect': '0.2', 'unit': 'percentage points', 'comparator': '', 'outcome': 'failure rate',
           'population': 'sites', 'geography': '', 'period': '', 'technology': 'widget', 'industry': '',
           'unit_of_analysis': 'region', 'design': 'observational', 'uncertainty': 'standard error 0.05',
           'horizon': 'long-run', 'mechanisms': []}

    seen = {}
    real = g.resolve_attribution

    def spy(ref, policy=P.JOURNAL_ONLY):
        seen['ref'] = ref
        return real(ref, policy)

    monkeypatch.setattr(g, 'resolve_attribution', spy)
    rejects = {k: 0 for k in ('span_not_in_source', 'span_too_short', 'offset_roundtrip_failed',
                              'span_binding_failed', 'span_binding_mismatch',
                              'source_policy_inadmissible')}
    from collections import defaultdict
    rejects = defaultdict(int)
    EM.gate_card(raw, view, chunk, paper, pw, EM.MiningContract(), rejects,
                 graph=g, source_policy=P.ANY_VERSION)
    # The gate reached resolve_attribution with a BINDING dict — carrying span offsets — never a str.
    assert 'ref' in seen, f'gate_card refused before resolving: {dict(rejects)}'
    assert isinstance(seen['ref'], dict)
    assert 'span_start' in seen['ref'] and 'span_end' in seen['ref']
    assert seen['ref']['manifestation_id'] == mid


# ── metamorphic generality: identifiers & subject swapped, structure held ────────────────────────

@pytest.mark.parametrize('subj', [
    dict(title='A Randomized Trial of the Antihypertensive Compound', authors=('Ng', 'Okafor'),
         byline='By Wei Ng and Chidi Okafor', venue='New England Journal of Medicine',
         requested='10.1056/clin-A', foreign='10.1056/clin-B'),
    dict(title='On the Interpretation of the Contract Formation Doctrine', authors=('Reyes', 'Silva'),
         byline='By Ana Reyes and Ivo Silva', venue='Harvard Law Review',
         requested='10.2307/legal-A', foreign='10.2307/legal-B'),
    dict(title='A Transformer Architecture for Program Synthesis Tasks', authors=('Park', 'Vasquez'),
         byline='By Jin Park and Luis Vasquez', venue='NeurIPS Proceedings',
         requested='10.5555/cs-A', foreign='10.5555/cs-B'),
])
def test_different_work_is_structural_across_unrelated_subjects(subj):
    # SAME structure (a foreign front-matter DOI), completely different subject/identifiers each time.
    g, m = _one([_row(subj['requested'], subj['foreign'], title=subj['title'],
                      authors=subj['authors'], byline=subj['byline'], venue=subj['venue'])])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'DIFFERENT_WORK'
    att = g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION)
    assert att.reason_code == RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
    assert att.admitted is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize('subj', [
    dict(title='A Randomized Trial of the Antihypertensive Compound', authors=('Ng', 'Okafor'),
         byline='By Wei Ng and Chidi Okafor', venue='New England Journal of Medicine', doi='10.1056/clin-A'),
    dict(title='On the Interpretation of the Contract Formation Doctrine', authors=('Reyes', 'Silva'),
         byline='By Ana Reyes and Ivo Silva', venue='Harvard Law Review', doi='10.2307/legal-A'),
    dict(title='A Transformer Architecture for Program Synthesis Tasks', authors=('Park', 'Vasquez'),
         byline='By Jin Park and Luis Vasquez', venue='NeurIPS Proceedings', doi='10.5555/cs-A'),
])
def test_same_work_admits_structurally_across_unrelated_subjects(subj):
    g, m = _one([_row(subj['doi'], subj['doi'], title=subj['title'], authors=subj['authors'],
                      byline=subj['byline'], venue=subj['venue'])])
    assert m.profile['semantic_binding'] == 'SAME_WORK'
    assert g.resolve_attribution(m.id, P.ANY_VERSION).admitted is True
